r: t̤o, e, t̤ā, t̤āʼě-mǒhmala, t̤āʼě-g̠air-manqūt̤a, te, t̤abar-zad, tabar-zad, t, ṭa, ḍa, ṛa
def: (called t̤āʼě-mǒhmala or t̤āʼě-g̠air-manqūt̤a), the twenty-second letter of the Urdū alphabet (the sixteenth letter of the Arabic, from which it is taken). The letter is purely Arabic or Semitic; but it is sometimes used in certain Persian words for an original ت te (e.g. t̤abar-zad for tabar-zad, 'sugar-candy'). In Arabic it is a strongly articulated palatal t; but in Hindūstānī and Persian its sound is hardly distinguishable from that of ت te. As a numeral it stands for 9; and in almanacs it represents the sign Capricorn. In Urdū manuscripts it is commonly used instead of the four dots or points over the letters ٿ ṭa, ڐ ḍa, and ڙ ṛa (e.g. ٹ &c.).
more: called tāë mohmala , or tāë gair-manqūtā . The 16th letter of the Arabic alphabet. It does not occur in pure Persian; but, in compliance with Arabic orthography, it is sometimes introduced in Persian words which were originally written with ت In abjad it stands for 9, and in almanacs for the sign Capricorn.

the sixteenth letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not occur in pure Persian; but, in compliance with Arabic orthography, it is sometimes introduced in Persian words which were originally written with ت; as, طبرزد for تبرزد, Sugar-candy; طبرخون for تبرخون, Red sanders. It has no corresponding character in Sanskrit. Its genuine sound is stronger and harsher than that of ت. As a numeral, in the numerical application of the Arabic alphabet, it stands for 9: and, in almanacks it represents the sign Capricorn. When this letter is the first radical of Arabic verbs, the servile ت of the measures تفاعل ,تفعّل and افتعال may be changed into ط, which is joined by idg̠ẖām with the ط of the root; and, if the first letter thus becomes quiescent, a hamza is prefixed; as, for تطہر tat̤ahhara اطہر it̤t̤ahhara.

def: s.m. = تولا tolā, q.v. s.m. A weight of 12 (or 16) māshas (esp. of gold or silver), the weight of the Sicca rupee, or about 179⅔ grains; (the tola is reckoned in books at 16 māshas of 6½ grains each, and weighs therefore 184 grains Troy; in practice, however, it is calculated at 12 māshas, jeweller's weight, and weighs nearly 210 grains); — a weigher of coins, &c.; weighman
more: s. m. Same as تولا tolā, q. v.

def: s.m. A quarter or district of a town; a part of a town inhabited by men of one class, sect, or trade; — a set (of anything), a complete assortment; — a piece of rock, a stone, a large pebble; a knock, stroke.
more: s. m. A quarter or particular part of a town.
s. m. (dakh.) A gang, group, caravan ( Harris ).

def: s.m. A weight (see tolā); a weighman.
s.f. A band, body, &c. ( = ṭol); a quarter of a town, &c. ( = ṭolā); a heavy stone.
more: s. f. A company, a society, a crowd.

ety: tol˚ = Prk. तोल(इ) or तोले(इ) = S. तोल(ति) or तोलय(ति), rt. तुल्
def: v.t. To weigh; to balance; to ponder, deliberate; to judge, estimate; to confront one another (as two armies).
more: v. a. S. तुल् to weigh. 1. To weigh; to put in the scales. 1. Sach bol, pūrā tol . Prov. Speak the truth and give full weight. 2. Sāhab merā bānyā, banaj kare, behvār, Bin ḍāṅḍī, bin pālṛe tole jag sansār . Dohā. My Lord's a store-supplier great, in merchandize he deals, Nor beams nor scales, in His own hands this great world weighs and feels. 2. To ponder; deliberate; judge; estimate.
v. a. 1. To weigh, to balance. 2. To confront (as, two armies). تولنا نظرون مين taulnā naz̤roṅ meṅ, To estimate the worth of any one.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀮𑀤𑀺 (taladi), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *talati. Compare Punjabi ਤਲਣਾ (talṇā), Gujarati તળવું (taḷvũ), Marathi तळणे (taḷṇe), Odia ତଳିବା (taḷibā).

ety: for A. طعمة, n. of un. fr. t̤ǒʻm, inf. n. of طعم 'to eat'
def: s.m. Food; dinner, a meal, repast; bait (thrown to fish, &c.); grain, &c. (thrown to birds).

s.m. A myriad, ten-thousand; a sum of money equal to ten thousand Arabic silver drachmas (which are about one-third less than those of the Greeks); a sum of fifteen dollars and a half; districts into which a kingdom is divided (each being supposed to furnish 10,000 fighting men).
more: s. m. 1. A myriad, 10,000. 2. A sum of money equal to 10,000 Arabic silver drachmas (which are about one third less than those of the Greeks): also, a sum equal to fifteen dollars and a half. 3. Districts, into which a kingdom is divided, each being supposed to furnish 10,000 fighting men. See تمن (When the city of Samarkand, for example, is put down for seven tomāns, it implies that she must hold 70,000 men ready for the field on the requisition of her sovereign. Richards ).

def: s.m. An iron club or crow; a lance; a javelin; name of a class of Rājpūts ( = tuwar, taṅwar); a kind of metre consisting of four lines of nine syllables each.
s.f. = tūmṛī and toṅbṛī, qq.v. s.f. A hollow gourd, &c. ( = tumbī; tūmbṛī, tombṛī).
s.f. = tūmṛī and toṅbṛī, qq.v. s.f. A hollow gourd, &c. ( = tumbī; tūmbṛī, tombṛī).
s.m. Name of a class of Rājpūts ( = tomar).
s.m. The gourd Lagenaria vulgaris in a dry state; the same with the inner part scooped out (used by mendicants to carry water in, &c., and also as floats in swimming, &c.).
more: s. m. ( तुम्बी ) A hollowed gourd. (Cucurbita lagenaria).

def: s.f. = tomṛā, q.v.; (tūmṛī) a musical instrument made of the gourd, a kind of pipe (used by snake-charmers and jugglers); the snout of an alligator, or crocodile, &c. (so called from its resemblance to the shape of the gourd); (tomṛī or tūmṛī) a lantern made of a hollowed gourd, or of a hollowed water-melon, or of earth, &c.; a kind of firework (a small earthen pot filled with powder, &c.; syn. phul-jharī).
more: s. f. ( तुम्बी ) A hollowed gourd. (Cucurbita lagenaria).

def: adv. In that manner, so, thus; then, in that case ( = to; tab, q.v.); also = tyoṅ, q.v.
more: adv. So, in that manner, then. ¶ taun (Sans. तद् ) pron. correl. That. ¶ tūṅ, pron. 2d pers. sing. Thou: see تو tū.
s. (dakh.) Corpulency, fatness: adj. Bulky. تون دار ton-dār, adj. Gorbellied, lusty, corpulent ( Harris ).
subs: adv. In that very manner, exactly so; — exactly then, at that very instant, there and then; then even.

def: v.t. = ṭohnā, q.v. v.t. To feel for, to feel, touch, handle; to grope for, search for; to trace, hunt for ( = ḍhūṅḍhnā).
s. m. Hocus pocus, juggling.
s. f. Hocus pocus, juggling.
s.m. Charm, spell, enchantment (cf. ṭoṭkā), necromancy, magic, witchcraft; fascination; a marriage song
more: 1. s. m. Enchantment, fascination. 2. v. n. To feel, to grope, to handle.
subs: (-ke), To nullify or ward off enchantments or spells
s.m. An enchanter, magician, necromancer, wizard, conjurer, juggler ( = ṭonhā)
s.m. = ṭonā-ṭānī, s.f. Charms and spells; hocus-pocus, juggling.

def: s.m. = tumbā; tomṛā, qq.v. s.m. The gourd Lagenaria vulgaris; a hollowed gourd (in which mendicants carry water, &c.; see toṅbā, tomṛī, tūmṛī, &c.).
more: n. m. See تومرَي Kaṛvī jā kī bel hai, lambe jā ke pāt; Phal jāke tīrath kare, rahe sant ke pās . Riddle. Ans. Tūṅbā a calabash. A creeper bitter to the taste, its leaves are long, Its fruit makes pilgrimage with holy men along.
s. m. A hollowed gourd, the rind of a gourd in which beggars carry water, &c.

def: s.f. = tumbī; tomṛī, tūmṛī, qq.v. s.f. A long white gourd, Lagenaria vulgaris; a small hollow gourd (in which medicants carry water, &c.; cf. tumbā); a kind of pipe or musical instrument (made of the gourd) played on by snake-charmers, jugglers, &c. ( = tumṛī; tāṅbī, toṅbī).
more: n. f. 1. A small gourd. 2. A musical instrument played on by jugglers and snake-charmers. toṅbī hāth meṅ lenā , v. n. To beg as mendicants.
s. f. 1. ( तुम्बी ) A small تونبا tūmbā, q. v. 2. See تومري tūmrī. 3. A kind of pipe (chiefly used by those who exhibit snakes).

def: s.m. = tumbā; tomṛā, qq.v. s.m. The gourd Lagenaria vulgaris; a hollowed gourd (in which mendicants carry water, &c.; see toṅbā, tomṛī, tūmṛī, &c.).

s.f. = tumbī; tomṛī, tūmṛī, qq.v. s.f. A long white gourd, Lagenaria vulgaris; a small hollow gourd (in which medicants carry water, &c.; cf. tumbā); a kind of pipe or musical instrument (made of the gourd) played on by snake-charmers, jugglers, &c. ( = tumṛī; tāṅbī, toṅbī).
s.f. A large belly, a pot-belly; corpulence.
more: n. f. S. तुन्द 1. A pot-belly; a large belly. Parāī toṅd kā ghūṅsā . Prov. A blow in another's stomach! (It does n't touch me!) 2. Corpulence.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *तोंद (toṃda), तुंद n (tuṃda), from Sanskrit तुन्द n (tunda).

def: adj. & s.m. Pot-bellied, gorbellied, corpulent; — a gorbellied person.
adj. & s.m. Pot-bellied, gorbellied, corpulent; — a gorbellied person.
adj. & s.m. = toṅdailā.
more: adj. S. तुन्दिल Pot-bellied (پيتَل). Baiṭhtā hai jab tuṅdīlā Shaikh ā-kar bazm meṅ , Inshā. Ek baṛā maṭkā sā rahtā hai shikam āge dharā . When this pot-bellied Shaikh doth in th'assembly sit him down, A great big jar one seems to see before his stomach shewn.

def: adj. & s.m. = toṅdailā.
s.f. The navel ( = tonḍī).
more: n. f. 1. W. The navel ( sūṅḍī ). 2. E. ( toṅdī ) The finger tips. Toṅdioṅ se bhī jo chūā ho, to toṅdī gal jāë! Wom. If I ever so much as touched the child with the tips of my fingers, may they rot!
n. f. S. तुन्दी belly. The navel.

def: s.f. A finger-tip.
s.f. The navel.
s.f. The navel ( = toṅdī).
adj. & s.m. = toṅdailā.
s. Necromancer.
s.f. Point; end, &c. = ṭoṅk, q.v. s.f. lit. 'A beak or bill'; point (of a weapon, tool, pen, &c.); end, extremity, limit, extreme (of a building, or a town or village, or a strip of land, &c.).
s. m. A juggling man, a conjurer.
s.m. One who casts spells, a necromancer, sorcerer, wizard, magician, conjurer, juggler; one who professes to cure diseases by means of charms or superstitious remedies.
s.f. Enchantress, sorceress, witch, female conjurer, &c. (see ṭonhā).
s. f. A juggling woman, a female conjurer, a witch.
adv. Opp. of joṅhīṅ . S. तदाहि At the very moment; instantly; at once. Joṅhīṅ woh āyā, toṅhīṅ maiṅ bhāgā .
adv. Exactly then.
s.f. lit. 'A beak or bill'; point (of a weapon, tool, pen, &c.); end, extremity, limit, extreme (of a building, or a town or village, or a strip of land, &c.).
s.f. = ṭok, and ṭhoṅg, qq.v. s.f. Questioning, interrogating; challenge; check, prevention, hindrance, obstruction; parole, watchword; the influence of an evil eye
subs: v.n. To speak after due inquiry, to speak from experience.

def: s.f. The beak or bill of a bird ( = ćoṅć)
subs: v.n. lit. 'To become like a bill'; to become rigid, be benumbed or cramped.

def: n. f. A beak; bill ( choṅch ). ṭoṅṭ baṅdhnā , v. n. To be benumbed or cramped.
s.m. = ṭoṭā, q.v.; — adj. Handless. s.m. A parrot; — pet, darling (a term of endearment applied to children)
more: s. m. 1. A cracker, a serpent in fireworks. 2. A joint of bamboo, &c. 3. A cartridge. 4. adj. Handless.
subs: v.n. To talk like a parrot, to prattle or talk sweetly
(-ke, or hathoṅ-ke), To lose one's power of prating, to be struck dumb, to be confounded
v.n. To turn away one's faithless eyes (to another), to withdraw the affections (from), to become indifferent (to).

def: n. m. See تَوتَا
s.f. A spout
subs: ṭoṅṭī-wālā, adj. Having a spout; — ṭoṅṭī-wālā loṭā, s.m. An ewer.

def: n. f. A spout. Aisan ḍhikṭā marlas kī kapār phāṭ-ke ṭoṅṭī nīar rakat chalat . Bhoj. He hit him with a shard so that he split open his head and the blood began to flow.
s.m. Charm, &c. ( = ṭonā); — a species of hawk.
more: s. m. 1. A kind of hawk. 2. Enchantment: see ٿونا ṭonā.

ety: T. tob, top, orig. 'a round mass' = S. stūpa
def: s.f. A gun, cannon, field-piece; mortar, howitzer; — (fig.) a fat or corpulent person
more: n. f. 1. A gun; cannon; field piece; mortar; howitzer. 2. ( Met .) A very fat; corpulent person.

s. f. A cannon, a gun. توپ انداز top-andāz, A cannoneer. توپ چلانا-چهوڙنا-داغنا-مارنا or لگانا top-chalānā, -chhoṛnā, -dāg̠ẖnā, -lagānā, or -mārnā, To cannonade. توپ خانه top-ḵẖāna, s. m. A park of artillery, the place where cannon and artillery stores are kept, an arsenal.
subs: v.t. To blow away from a gun ( = top-se uṛānā)
s.m. Artillery; the artillery (in an army); a battery, a park of artillery; the place where cannon and artillery stores are kept, an arsenal
s.m. Fire-arms, muskets, rifles (as opposed to cannon)
v.t. To blow away from a gun; to condemn to be shot
top mārnā, v.n. = top ćalānā, q.v.

def: s.m. Heap, pile; cluster; a Buddhist monument, a tumulus erected over a sacred spot (as the top of Manikiāla in the Panjāb); a cluster of trees, a grove, plantation, garden.
s.m. A Buddhist monument (a kind of tumulus erected over sacred relics of the great Buddha, or on spots consecrated as the scenes of his acts).
more: s. m. 1. A hat, cap, helmet, head, cover, thimble. 2. Bait. 3. A stitch.

def: s.m. A covering for the head; a cornered cap (lined with cotton) which covers the ears and the back of the head; a kind of hood; a hat, helmet; cover, head, or cap (of a thing); a thimble; a drop; bait; a long stitch (such as is used in basting or tacking, cf. ṭappā).
n. m. A battery; an artillery park. top se uṛānā , v. a. To blow away from a gun; condemn to be shot.

s.m. A covering for the head; a cornered cap (lined with cotton) which covers the ears and the back of the head; a kind of hood; a hat, helmet; cover, head, or cap (of a thing); a thimble; a drop; bait; a long stitch (such as is used in basting or tacking, cf. ṭappā).
more: s. m. A helmet, cap, hat.

def: n. m. 1. A cap which covers the ears, ( kan-ṭop ); a nightcap. 2. A drop.
v.t. = tupānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be buried, to have (a person or thing) buried, covered, or concealed.
more: (caus. of توپنا) v. a. To have buried, to cause to cover.

def: s.m. A conductor of artillery; a commissary of ordnance; a bombardier; a cannonier.
more: n. m. A cannonier. Dekhā bhālā topchī, aur chaprā Saīyad hoë . Prov. A gunner known, a Sayad perforce.
s. m. A conductor of artillery, a commissary of ordnance, bombardier.

def: n. f. H. topnā to cover. 1. A hat; cap. 2. The cap of a telescope, etc.; a cap or gun-cap. 3. The nut of the penis . ṭopī-vālā , n. m. 1. One who wears a hat. Taiṅ to merā man har līnā, ṭopī-wāle saṅvaryā . Wom. song. 2. A European. Kamāve dhotī-vālā, uṛāve ṭopī-vālā . Prov. The naked loins-clad earn, the black hat-wearers spend.
s.f. A hat, cap; cap (of a gun); cover or cap (as of a telescope, &c.); head or top (of a thing)
more: s. f. A hat. ٿوپـَي والا ṭopī-wālā, One who wears a hat, or an animal with a comb, crest, &c. It is used to express the Persian or Moghal troops, and also those of Europeans.
subs: s.m. One who wears a hat; a European, (also, in former times, a Persian or a Mogul soldier); an animal with a comb, or a crest, &c.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀝𑁄𑀧𑀺𑀆 (ṭopiā, “helmet”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-), related to Sanskrit टोपिका (ṭopikā).

def: v.t. To cover with a top or mound, to bury; to cover; to conceal.
more: v. a. S. स्थापित To cover; bung up; bury under ground. Nigoḍḍe, tujhāṅ gaḍhe māṅ toppūṅ . Rus. Wom. You good-for-nothing wretch! I'll bury you in a hole.
v. a. See topnā . 1. To sow by hand; to put down a young tree or cutting; to plant. 2. To baste.
v. a. To bury, to cover.

def: v.t. To cover with earth, to bury; to set (a tree or cutting), to plant; to sow with the hand; to hang, suspend; to baste, to tack (in which sense it is connected with thāp, q.v.).
s.m. A fly; — a kind of pigeon.
more: s. m. 1. A fly. 2. A kind of pigeon.

def: s.f. A net thrown over a woman's pālkī.
more: n. m. S. तुड to break. 1. Crack; breach; rent; furrow ( phūṭ ). 2. Irrigation effected by breaking down the ridges of water courses and letting the water flood the field. 3. The force or velocity of a tide or current. 4. Reply; refutation; answer. 5. Counterblow; defence; remedy; cure. 1. Is pech kā ham ko toṛ batāo . 2. Is davā kā kyā toṛ hai? 6. Height; climax. Mele yā garmī kā toṛ . 7. Whey. Dahī kā toṛ . toṛ phoṛ, toṛ tāṛ , n. m. 1. Wholesale destruction; ravage; devastation. 2. Enticement; winning over. toṛ joṛ, joṛ toṛ , 1. See ادهيرَبن 1, 2. Joṛī jo un ne tujh se, to toṛī raqīb se, Inshā, tū apne yār ke ye toṛ joṛ dekh! Inshā. To you attached, she's broken with your rival, see, Insha, to make and break alike your mistress free! 2. Shifts; resources. 3. Combinations; arrangements; construction (of a sentence).
s. f. 1. A kind of pulse. See توار. 2. A net thrown over a womanʼs pālkī. 3. (in the eastern dialect) pron. 2d. p. sing. gen. Thy, thine.

ety: Prk. तोड़ो, S. त्रोटः; see toṛnā
def: s.m. Breaking; break, interruption; fracture, rupture; breach, rent, fissure; breaking or injuring power (of a gun, &c.); force or velocity (of a current, &c.); method of warding off or meeting (a blow, &c.), guard, defence; efficacy, effect, virtue (of a medicine, &c.); effective or telling rejoinder, answer, refutation; turning point, height, climax (of heat, cold, disease, &c.); irrigation of fields by flooding (i.e. by breaking down a dam, or the ridges of water-courses); whey; — a net thrown over a child's cradle, or a woman's pālkī
more: s. m. 1. The breach made by a gun, &c. 2. The strength of a current. 3. Whey. توڙپهوڙ toṛ-phoṛ (from توڙنا and پهوڙنا) s. m. Breaking, plain speaking. توڙتاڙ toṛ-tāṛ, s. m. 1. Breaking and destroying. 2. Plain speaking. توڙجوڙ toṛ-joṛ (from توڙنا and جوڙنا) s. m. 1. Cutting out (as, cloth by a tailor). 2. Arranging a speech, &c. توڙدينا toṛ denā, To break, to spoil. توڙڐالنا toṛ-ḍālnā, To break and destroy, to pull down. توڙکرنا toṛ karnā, To make a breach (as, a ball). توڙلينا toṛ lenā, To gather, to pluck (fruit).
s. f. A net work thrown over a womanʼs pālkī, &c. See تور tor.
subs: toṛ-tāṛ, s.m. Breaking and smashing; breaking to pieces; fracture, rupture; breach; injury, damage, destruction, ravage, havoc; effectiveness; plain-speaking; winning over, allurement, enticement

(and joṛ-toṛ), s.m. Breaking and joining; breaking (with one) and taking up (with another); taking to pieces and reconstructing (a machine); arrangement (of words in composition), construction (of a sentence), combination, cutting out and putting together (a garment); contriving, managing, effecting through shifts and expedients; deliberation, planning; plans, contrivances, schemes, devices, expedients, shifts
(-meṅ), To make a breach (in, as a ball)

ety: Prk. तुह+gen. aff. rā; see to and tu
def: adj. (dialec.), Thy, thine( = terā).
s.m. = توره tora, q.v. adj. (dialec.), Thy, thine( = terā).

ety: prob. an Aryan word in a Semitic form
def: s.m. A tray or dish of various meats; a number of trays containing various dishes of food (presented by the rich on marriage and other occasions); — a nobleman; a minister; — pride, haughtiness, conceit, airs (cf. toṛā)
more: n. m. 1. A tray or dish of various meats; a number of trays containing various dishes of food, presented by rich men on marriage occasions, etc. 2. Wom. Ostentation; pride. torā-bandī , n. f. The ceremony of sending out trays of food as presents. torah lagnā , v. n. Wom. To be vain or proud. Ab to unheṅ bhī torā lag gayā. sharā torah , Wom. n. m. Mah. law.

s. m. 1. A number of trays, containing various dishes of food, presented to others by great men. 2. A nobleman, a minister. 3. Pride. توره پوش tora-posh, s. m. A covering for dishes, &c. توره بندي tora-bandī, s. f. The arrangement or sending out of trays of food as presents.
subs: s.f. The arrangement or the sending out of trays of food as presents
(-ko), To become proud, couceited, or vain, &c.

def: s.m. Break, interruption, exhaustion, deficiency, scarcity, want; a piece of rope or cord; a plough-share; the match of a gun, a linstock; a bank, bar, an island (syn. ćar); a purse, a bag of one thousand rupees (in this sense prob. derived from S. tola+ka); a gold or silver chain for the neck, a collarette (usually consisting of four or five separate chains); a similar ornament for the ankles (usually corsisting of three chains); — name of a rāg or musical mode
more: s. m. 1. Scarcity, want. 2. A purse, a bag containing one thousand rupees. 3. The match of a gun. 4. A bank, an island, bar. 5. A ploughshare. 6. A piece of rope. 7. An ornament like a chain. توڙي دار toṛc-dār, A matchlock.
(or tūṛā, Harris ) s. m. A linstock.
subs: s.f. Wrenching and twisting: pinching and squeezing
s.m., toṛe-dār bandūk, s.f. A matchlock; a gun of great range or damaging power.

def: s.m. = toṛā, q.v. adj. (dialec.), Thy, thine( = terā).
s.m. A kind of anklet with bells (worn by women and children; cf. toṛā).
more: s. m. A ledge extending out from a wall, to keep off the weather, without pillars; eaves.

def: s.m. = ṭoṛā, q.v. adj. (dialec.), Thy, thine( = terā).
s.m. A ledge (without pillars) extending out from a wall (to keep off the weather); eaves; battens of a roof.
s.m. Custom, law, rule, authority, order, institute, regulation, enactment (e.g. sharʻ tore-kī zan hai).

ety: Prk. तुह+gen. aff. rā; see to and tu
def: adj. (dialec.), Thy, thine( = terā).
more: n. m. 1. Scarcity; dearth ( kamī ). Āj kal kāgaz kā toṛā hai . 2. A piece of rope. 3. A ploughshare. 4. The match of a gun; a linstock. 5. A purse; a bag containing one thousand rupees. Makān meṅ pachchīs toṛe kharch huë . 6. A gold or silver chain. 7. A bank; bar; an island. 8. A mode in music. toṛe-dār bandūq , n. f. A matchlock.
n. m. One of several small bells ( ghūṅgrū ) worn by children and women.
n. m. The battens between the beams.
s. (dakh.) A game of children, same as کبڐي kabaḍḍī (Qanoon-e-islam).

def: A matchlock.

ety: toṛa+l = Prk. डो = S. रः or लः
def: s.m. A large thick ring of gold or silver, &c. worn on the wrist or ankle.
more: s. m. A large thick ring worn on the wrist.

def: s.m. An arch, gateway; the ornamented arch of a door or gateway; the decorations of a gateway; strings of flowers suspended across gateways on festive occasions (syn. bandanwār).
more: s. m. Strings of flowers suspended across gateways on publick festivals.

def: v.t. = tuṛānā, tup wānā, q.v. s.m. Boiled rice kept in cold water over night and used next morning, rice-gruel (syn. kānjī).

def: v.t. To break, tear, rend, burst, split, crack; to interrupt, cut off, stop, discontinue, abolish, to exhaust; to sever, sunder (a tie, or friendship, &c.); to destroy, demolish; to ruin (a person); to violate (a vow), infringe (a canon); to break into (a house), force or break open (a lock); to break up (ground), to plough; to pluck or gather (fruit, &c.); to stretch, strain, exercise (the body, as by gymnastics); to take, capture, conquer (a stronghold); to demolish (an argument), to refute, confute; to change (money); to reduce (in Arith.)
subs: v.t. 'To break and join'; to take to pieces and reconstruct; to make and mar
(intens. of toṛnā), v.t. To break to pieces, to break and destroy, to pull down, to demolish, &c.

v.t. To wrench and twist; to pinch and squeeze

def: v. a. 1. To break; crack; rend; shatter; smash. Samman, ḍorā prem kā jin ṭoṛo chhiṭkāë; Toṛe par jo joṛ ho, bīch gāṅṭh paṛ jāë . Dohā. Sweetheart, affection's cord once snapped and rent, However joined again the knot is prominent. 2. To break or plough; make furrows. 3. To force or break open; commit burglary. 4. To violate; deflour; dishonor. 5. To cut, or draw off; to exhaust. Anjan se pānī toṛ dīyā . 6. To eat the bread of idleness. Roṭyāṅtoṛnā . 7. To prepare; make up. Baṛyāṅ toṛnā . 8. To break (a bank). 9. To break (a promise, vow, etc). 10. To kill. Kanth, bachan mere suno, billī ne karī ghāt; Kanṭh pakaṛ, panjā dabā, toṛ diyā ek sāt . Padmāvat. 11. To pluck; gather. Mircheṅ toṛnā . 12. To pull down; demolish. Dīvār toṛnā . 13. To refute; confute; disprove; set at naught. Dalīl toṛ dī . 14. To break; violate; infringe. Lāë us but ko iltijā kar-ke, Kufr toṛā Khudā Khudā kar-ke . 15. To break or quarrel with; fall out. * Kadū 1 kī dostī, ek pal 2 meṅ torī, 3 Tum aise begunoṅ 4 ko kyā karele 5 ? Zillā, q. v. 2. Sab toṛeṅ, merā Rab na toṛe! Wom. Prov. All may break from me, my God be with me! 3. Prīt kareṅ so bāure, kar-ke toṛeṅ chhail, Gal meṅ rassā ḍāl-ke oṛ nibhāveṅ bail . Kabīr. Who love are fools, who love and break are rakes, A rope thrown round his neck the ox submission makes. 16. To discontinue; stop; cancel; annul; abolish. 17. To defeat; conquer; capture; take. Qilā toṛā . 18. To draw; bring, win, gain, over. Gavāh ṭor līyā . 19. To stretch; strain; exercise. Khushī meṅ muddatoṅ tak ham ne badan ko toṛā, Sau gul-badan ke tan ko man-māntā maṛoṛā . Nazīr. My muscles I have trained for years in many a wrestling mill, A hundred beauteous forms have yielded to my wanton will. 20. To change money. Rupayā toṛnā. toṛnā joṛnā , v. n. lit . To break and join. * All five are the names of vegetables. 1. Old. 2. moment. 3. broke. 4. unskilled. 5. made. To make and mar. toṛnā maṛoṛnā , v. n. To pinch and squeeze. Agar yūṅhī toṛā maṛoṛī rahegī; To kāhe ko aṅgyā nigoṛī rahegī . Wom. If all this tierce and thrust for long goes on This wretched boddice is for ever done.
s. (dakh.) See تقاضا taḳāẓā. توڙني دينا يالگانا toṛnī-denā or -lagānā, See تنگ کرنا tang-karnā, under تنگ tang.

A parrot, stork, and quails two here, These four I captive took, my dear, I chewed and drank their blood yfere. 2. Bāteṅ kare munyā kī sī, āṅkheṅ badle tote kī sī . Prov. To wash and wash and drink the water from my feet, While I chastised him with my shoes, charm meet Give me, Jān Sāhab , my spouse to rule, I do entreat. 2. Kisī ne ṭoṭkā karne ko merā āṅchal katar līyā . Wom. Somebody has cut off a piece of my wrap to work a charm on me. 3. Ṭoṭkā kā kare ailū rahe? kā ailu, kā challū? . Mag. To fall out; befall; happen. 3. To fall or rain in torrents; to hail. Maze, aish o tarab, lazzat lage yūṅ ṭūṭ-kar girne, Ki jaise ṭūṭ-kar mevoṅ ke hoveṅ ḍher āṅdhī meṅ . Nazīr. Such pleasure and enjoyment with such sensuous joy outpoured, As in a storm great heaps of fruit upon the earth are showered. 4. To fall upon; rush upon; charge; assault; fly at; pounce upon. Har ān ṭūṭ paṛtā hai roṭī ke khuān par . Nazīr. 5. To spend freely; to lavish; squander. 6. To be unrealized; to fall into arrears. 7. To be reduced to poverty. 8. To be lost, spent; gone; ruined. 9. To become weak, feeble, infirm. 10. To feel pain in the joints; to be ill. 11. To cease to exist; to die. Ṭūṭī kī būṭī nahīṅ . Prov. Death…

s.f. A collection or assemblage of three, a triad; the three sacred fires collectively (i.e. the southern, household, and sacrificial fires); the second yug or silver age of the Hindūs (comprising 1,296,000 years)
more: n. m. The second of the four ages, called the silver age comprising 1,296,000 years.
s. f. The name of the second or silver age, comprising 1,296,000 years.
subs: (˚tā+ag˚), s.m. The three sacred fires collectively ( = agni-tretā); one who has preserved the three sacred fires.

def: Trimurti
adj. Three (used in comp.)
s.f. lit. 'Triple-braid'; the place (now called Allahabad) where the Ganges unites with the Jamnā and is supposed to receive underground the Sarasvatī; the confluence of three sacred rivers.
more: n. f. lit . Three braids of hair. The confluence of three sacred rivers, especially that of the Ganges, Jamnā, and the mythical Saraswati under ground at Allahabad. 1. Sīs kes bin, chuṭyā tīn, augan let parāye chhīn, Jo jāë unke darbār, tāke mūṅḍ na rākhe bār . Riddle. Ans. Tribenī . A hairless head, yet topknots three, your sins it takes away, All who unto its courts repair bald-headed come away. 2. Gangā tirbenī, baikunṭh kī nasainī . Bhajan. The three-forked Ganges, the ladder to Heaven.
s. f. The conflux of three sacred rivers; especially, that of the Ganges, Jamunā, and supposed Saraswatī under ground at Ilāhābād, which city is also so named.

def: adj. Having three curves or bends (as have many images of Kr̤ishna, i.e. with the legs, loins, and neck bent); standing awry; — s.m. A person standing awry.
adj. Having three curves or bends (as have many images of Kr̤ishna, i.e. with the legs, loins, and neck bent); standing awry; — s.m. A person standing awry.
more: adj. S. त्रि three, भङ्ग break. Standing awry, or bending (properly, with legs, loins, and neck bent).
adj. Standing awry, or bending (properly, with legs, loins and neck bent).

def: adj. & s.f. Having the position described under tri-bhaṅga, an epithet of Kr̤ishṇa
more: adj. Having the position of tribhangā (an epithet of Krishna). tribhangī chhand . ( Pros .) It has four lines, each of 10, + 8, + 8, + 6 = 32. No jagans ( ˘ ¯ ˘ ) allowed. Subordinate rhymes at each pause.
1. (from त्रिभंग ) adj. Having the position described under تربهنگا tribhangā, an epithet of Kr̤ish̤ṇa. 2. ( त्रिभंगी ) Name of a species of poetical measure.
subs: consisting of four lines, each of thirty-two syllabic instants.

def: s.m. The three worlds (heaven, earth, and the lower regions), the universe (syn. tri-lok)
more: n. m. The three worlds, heaven, earth, and the infernal regions; the universe.
s. m. The three worlds, viz. heaven, earth, and the infernal regions; the universe. تربهون اجاگر tribhuwan-ujāgar, adj. Splendour of the three worlds.
subs: s.m. The splendour of the three worlds
tri-bhuvan-nāth, s.m. Lord of the three worlds, epithet of Vishṇu
adj. Possessed of the three worlds, an epithet of Indra
s.f. The beauty of the three worlds; a woman of rare beauty.

def: adj. & s.m. Having three arms; three-sided, trilateral; — a three-sided figure, triangle.
s.f. The creeping plant Cissus pedata.

s.m. A wandering mendicant or devotee who has resigned worldly pursuits and carries three long bamboo staves tied together in his right hand; — the religious man who has his words, thoughts, and actions (or his mind, body, and speech) under control.
adj. & s.m. Thirty; — a name for the thirty-three deities (not including Brahmā, Vishṇu, and Śiva); — a god, a deity, an immortal; the abode of the thirty-three gods, heaven
subs: s.m. The food of the gods, ambrosia or amr̤it.

def: s.m. The Hindu Triad Brahmā, Vishṇu and Śiva.
adv. In three ways; in three parts; in three places; threefold, triply, trebly.
s.m. Disorder of the three humors of the body, vitiation of the bile, blood, and phlegm.
more: s. m. Effervescence of the three humours, viz. phlegm, bile, and atrabilis.

def: s.m. The aggregate of the three objects of existence, viz. virtue, pleasure, and wealth (syn. tri-varg, q.v.).
s.m. The country of the Trigartas, the modern Lahore.
s.f. A lascivious woman, a wanton.

s.m. The three qualities or constituents of nature and every existing thing; — any creature possessing those three qualities; — adj. Consisting of three threads or strings; threefold, triple, treble, thrice; possessing the three guṇas or qualities
subs: s.m. One who possesses the three guṇas or properties, an epithet of man.

tri-bhuvan-nāth, s.m. Lord of the three worlds, epithet of Vishṇu

def: s.f. The sine of ninety degrees, the radius (of a circle).
s.f. A triangular frame or bar across the mouth of a well (over which passes the rope of the bucket); a frame at the bottom of a well (on which the masonry rests).
adj. & s.m. Triple, threefold; forming a triad; — the aggregate of three, a triad; the chin-bone; the hip.
wety: From Classical Persian تُرک (turk), or conversely through Chagatai.

def: s.m. The three times or tenses, the past, present, and future; morning, noon, and evening
subs: (vulg. tri-kāla-gya), adj. & s.m. Knowing the three times, omniscient; — a divine sage; a name of Buddh; a deity
adj. & s.m. Seeing the past, present, and future; omniscient; — a Rishi or divine sage; a name of Buddha; the Omniscient.

def: s.m. A kind of fish (Silurus); a species of plant ( = tri-kaṭ; and gokhrū).
s.m. The plant Ruellia longifolia.
s.m. The aggregate of three spices, viz. black pepper, long pepper, and dry ginger.
adj. Thirsty.
adj. & s.m. Three-cornered, triangular; — a triangle; the vulva
more: n. f. A triangle; a trilateral figure. tri-kon-mitī ; A. ilm-i-musallas ; H. tikhūṅṭ biddiyā , n. m. Trigonometry.
subs: or tri-koṇ-mitī vidyā, s.f. The science of trigonometry (syn. ilm-ě-mus̤allas).

def: adj. & s.m. Having three peaks; — a three-peaked mountain; name of several mountains in India; — sea-salt prepared by evaporation.
s.m. The aggregate of three spices, viz. black pepper, long pepper, and dry ginger.
more: n. m. A mixture of pepper, long pepper and dry ginger.

def: adj. & s.m. Tri-ocular, three-eyed; — the three-eyed one, an epithet of Śiva.
more: n. m. lit . Having three eyes. An epithet of Shiv.
(having) three eyes; s. m. The Hindū deity, Siva.

def: s.m. The three worlds, i.e. the sky, atmosphere, and earth; or heaven, earth, and the lower region; — the universe; — an inhabitant of the three worlds
more: The three worlds, Heaven, earth, and the regions under the earth; the universe. trilokī-nāth , n. m. Lord of the universe.
m. ( त्रि Three, and लोक World). s. The three worlds, i. e. Heaven, earth, and the regions under the earth; the universe. ترلوکي ناتهـ trilokī-nāth, Lord of the universe.
subs: s.m. Lord of the three worlds, an epithet of Indra.

def: s.f. The universe ( = tri-lok), the aggregate of the three worlds
more: f. ( त्रि Three, and लोक World). s. The three worlds, i. e. Heaven, earth, and the regions under the earth; the universe. ترلوکي ناتهـ trilokī-nāth, Lord of the universe.
subs: s.m. Lord of the universe, an epithet of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.

def: s.m. lit. 'The three sweet substances, sugar, honey, and ghī'; a portion of the Rig-veda beginning with the word madhu; one who knows or recites the three verses beginning with madhu.
s.f. Three ways or paths; the meeting of three roads ( = tri-path; ti-rāhā).
adj. & s.m. Pertaining to, or consisting of a protracted vowel (i.e. a vowel lengthened to three mātrās or prosodial instants); a vowel so lengthened ( = plut).
adj. &c. = tri-mukh, q.v. adj. & s.m. Three-faced; having three mouths; — the confluence of three rivers.
